_provenance: Familial-ALS (fals) and sporadic-ALS (sals) shares and the specificity notes are from the GeneReviews ALS Overview (NBK1450, https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK1450/), Tables 2a and 2b. Genes outside those tables carry a brief classification with a representative PubMed citation found by literature search. Formal sensitivity and specificity are not reported per ALS gene; the shares below are the published attributable fractions and the notes are phenotype specificity, the field's closest measures. Compiled 2026-06-21.

C9orf72.spec: Also causes ALS-FTD, primary lateral sclerosis, PSP, and psychiatric features

SOD1.spec: Dominant or recessive; rapid progression with the p.Ala4Val variant

FUS.spec: Earlier average onset; more common in Asian cohorts

TARDBP.spec: Also causes ALS-FTD; mean onset about 53 years

ALS2.spec: Recessive; upper-motor-neuron predominant; juvenile onset; linked to PLS

ANG.spec: Primary phenotype is ALS; reported in European ancestry

CHCHD10.spec: Also linked to cerebellar ataxia or myopathy

DCTN1.spec: ALS; also Perry syndrome and distal motor neuropathy

FIG4.spec: Normal nerve conduction; limited lower-motor-neuron involvement

MATR3.spec: Also linked to primary lateral sclerosis and distal myopathy

NEK1.spec: ALS with or without cognitive impairment

OPTN.spec: Recessive or dominant; slow progression and long duration

SPG11.spec: Recessive; juvenile, very slow progression; consanguineous families

SQSTM1.spec: Also Paget disease of bone; seen with SOD1 and FUS variants

TBK1.spec: ALS with or without mild cognitive impairment

UBQLN2.spec: X-linked; affects both sexes; childhood to late-adult onset

VAPB.spec: ALS with or without postural tremor; mainly Brazilian and Japanese families

VCP.spec: Also Paget disease and parkinsonism; course can be rapid

UNC13A.spec: Risk gene for sporadic ALS and a disease-course modifier in familial ALS

ATXN2.spec: Intermediate-length CAG/polyglutamine repeat raises ALS risk; a full expansion causes spinocerebellar ataxia 2

KIF5A.spec: Identified as an ALS gene by genome-wide analysis (OMIM ALS25)

CCNF.spec: ALS with or without frontotemporal dementia (OMIM FTDALS5)

SIGMAR1.spec: Recessive juvenile ALS (OMIM ALS16); also distal hereditary motor neuropathy

TMEM106B.spec: Modifies risk in FTD and in C9orf72 ALS-FTD rather than causing ALS directly

NEFH.spec: Neurofilament heavy gene; rare variants reported in ALS, evidence limited

PRPH.spec: Peripherin gene; rare variants in sporadic ALS

ELP3.spec: Associated in an early genome-wide screen; replication limited

FGGY.spec: Reported as a risk factor for sporadic ALS in a genome-wide study

LRP12.spec: CGG repeat expansion reported in ALS

TRPM7.spec: Tested as an ALS-parkinsonism-dementia candidate; the association was not supported in the cited study
